title: Chylismia pterosperma
taxonomy: Chylismia pterosperma is a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. It belongs to the genus Chylismia, which is a group of western North American evening primroses. Chylismia pterosperma is further classified as a member of the order Myrtales and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Chylismia pterosperma is a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Chylismia pterosperma, also known as the sand verbena, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Chylismia pterosperma has been valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to treat anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Chylismia pterosperma is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
